item.title: skill
item.description: Retrieve knowledge from any published Distillary brain. Self-contained skill — give this to any agent with web access and a brain URL. No setup needed. Triggers on "query brain", "retrieve from brain", "use fractal brain", "look up in published brain".

item.title: The Art of Money Getting
item.description: The Art of Money Getting by P.T. Barnum (1880) — Public domain Written in 1880, P.T.

item.title: Brain
item.description: Brain This brain explores how wealth actually works—not as fortune or luck, but as a consequence of disciplined character operating through sound practices.

item.title: _suggestions
item.description: Vault Suggestions Generated by doctor agent 0 fixes applied | 0 ghost notes created | 0 frontier concepts | 0 remaining orphans Potentially over-extracted entities These entities are referenced by 0-1 claims.

item.title: Wealth is built by disciplined character operating through sound business practices, not by luck or shortcuts
item.description: Barnum’s complete System rests on four pillars that must all be present for Wealth to endure.

item.title: Brain and judgment matter more than mere labor
item.description: An employee who brings intellectual capacity and independent thinking to their work is far more valuable than one who merely offers physical labor.

item.title: Cascading purchases expand lifestyle permanently
item.description: Barnum illustrates with a gentleman’s story: his wife’s elegant sofa purchase (30,000) necessitated matching chairs, sideboards, carpets, and tables; this forced them to build a new house, and the yearly maintenance costs rose to 11,000.

item.title: Caution and boldness both necessary
item.description: The Rothschild’s maxim “Be cautious and bold” reconciles apparent contradiction.

item.title: Charity produces business benefits
item.description: The liberal man who gives to charity will command more patronage than the stingy miser, even if charity is pursued only as business policy.

item.title: Choose a vocation suited to aptitude and excel
item.description: Barnum argues that natural diversity in talents means different people have innate aptitudes; forcing someone into an unsuitable vocation creates lifelong struggle.
